---
title: "Theorems 7.4-7.5 — Residual variance and homoskedastic covariance consistency"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar_tendstoInMeasure"
chapter: 7
---

## Textbook statement

For the linear model $y_i = X_i^\top\beta + e_i$, Theorem 7.4 states that the
residual variance estimators converge to the population error variance:

$$
\hat\sigma_n^2 \xrightarrow{p} \sigma^2,
\qquad
s_n^2 \xrightarrow{p} \sigma^2.
$$

Theorem 7.5 then plugs this variance estimator into the homoskedastic
asymptotic covariance formula:

$$
\hat V_{\beta,n}^0 = s_n^2 \hat Q_n^{-1}
\xrightarrow{p}
V_\beta^0 = \sigma^2 Q^{-1}.
$$

In Lean, the assumptions are bundled as
[`ErrorVarianceConsistencyConditions μ X e`](../../assumptions/chapter7.qmd),
with the textbook-compatible alias
[`SampleVarianceAssumption74 μ X e`](../../assumptions/chapter7.qmd). The
linear model itself remains a separate pointwise hypothesis
`hmodel : ∀ i ω, y i ω = (X i ω) ⬝ᵥ β + e i ω`.

### Proof sketch

The variance estimator is handled first: show that the residual sum of squares
differs from the true error variance by terms that vanish in probability. Then
Theorem 7.5 combines two already-proved convergences:

1. $s_n^2 \to_p \sigma^2$ from Theorem 7.4.
2. $\hat Q_n^{-1} \to_p Q^{-1}$ from Theorem 7.1 plus continuous mapping.
3. Entrywise multiplication gives $s_n^2(\hat Q_n^{-1})_{ij} \to_p
   \sigma^2(Q^{-1})_{ij}$ for every $i,j$.
4. The matrix convergence is rebuilt from those coordinatewise limits.

## Lean statement

The relevant declarations live in
`HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean`:

| Lean endpoint | Role |
|---|---|
| [`olsSigmaSqHatStar_tendstoInMeasure_errorVariance`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1400) | unadjusted residual variance consistency |
| [`olsS2Star_tendstoInMeasure_errorVariance`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1506) | degrees-of-freedom residual variance consistency |
| [`homoskedasticAsymptoticCovariance`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1521) | population target $V_\beta^0=\sigma^2Q^{-1}$ |
| [`olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1526) | feasible estimator $\hat V_{\beta,n}^0=s_n^2\hat Q_n^{-1}$ |
| [`olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar_tendstoInMeasure`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter7Asymptotics/Consistency.lean#L1535) | Theorem 7.5 consistency |

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Hansen Theorem 7.5: consistency of the homoskedastic plug-in covariance estimator.">theorem olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar_tendstoInMeasure</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The sample measure is finite, enough for convergence in measure.">{μ : Measure Ω} [IsFiniteMeasure μ]</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Regressor, error, and outcome processes.">{X : ℕ → Ω → (k → ℝ)} {e : ℕ → Ω → ℝ} {y : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Bundled Theorem 7.4 variance-estimator assumptions.">(h : ErrorVarianceConsistencyConditions μ X e)</span> <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The true coefficient vector.">(β : k → ℝ)</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Pointwise linear model yᵢ = Xᵢ'β + eᵢ.">(hmodel : ∀ i ω, y i ω = (X i ω) ⬝ᵥ β + e i ω)</span> :
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Conclusion: the feasible matrix estimator s² Qhat⁻¹ converges in probability to σ² Q⁻¹.">TendstoInMeasure μ
      (fun n ω =>
        olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar
          (stackRegressors X n ω) (stackOutcomes y n ω))
      atTop (fun _ => homoskedasticAsymptoticCovariance μ X e)</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

### Reading the statement

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `ErrorVarianceConsistencyConditions μ X e` | Assumption 7.4 package | **Hypothesis:** WLLN and moment inputs for residual variance consistency, linked on the [Chapter 7 assumption page](../../assumptions/chapter7.qmd). |
| `β : k → ℝ` | $\beta \in \mathbb{R}^k$ | **Given:** the fixed population coefficient. |
| `hmodel` | $y_i=X_i^\top\beta+e_i$ | **Hypothesis:** the model connecting outcomes to regressors and errors. |
| `olsS2Star` | $s_n^2$ | The degrees-of-freedom adjusted residual variance estimator. |
| `olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar` | $s_n^2\hat Q_n^{-1}$ | The totalized feasible homoskedastic covariance estimator. |
| `homoskedasticAsymptoticCovariance μ X e` | $\sigma^2 Q^{-1}$ | The population covariance target. |

## Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

The proof of Theorem 7.5 is a clean Slutsky-style composition.

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Name the scalar variance estimator so the coordinate products are readable.">let s2 : ℕ → Ω → ℝ := fun n ω =>
    olsS2Star (stackRegressors X n ω) (stackOutcomes y n ω)</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Name the inverse sample Gram matrix.">let invGram : ℕ → Ω → Matrix k k ℝ := fun n ω =>
    (sampleGram (stackRegressors X n ω))⁻¹</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Theorem 7.4 supplies s²ₙ →p σ².">have hs2 := olsS2Star_tendstoInMeasure_errorVariance
    (μ := μ) (X := X) (e := e) (y := y) h β hmodel</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Theorem 7.1's Gram inverse result supplies Qhat⁻¹ →p Q⁻¹.">have hInv := sampleGramInv_stackRegressors_tendstoInMeasure_popGramInv
    (μ := μ) (X := X) (e := e) h.toSampleMomentAssumption71</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="For each matrix entry, multiply the scalar variance convergence by the matching inverse-Gram coordinate convergence.">have hEntry : ∀ i j : k,
      TendstoInMeasure μ
        (fun n ω => s2 n ω * invGram n ω i j)
        atTop
        (fun _ => errorVariance μ e * ((popGram μ X)⁻¹) i j) := by
    intro i j
    have hInvCoord : TendstoInMeasure μ
        (fun n ω => invGram n ω i j)
        atTop (fun _ => ((popGram μ X)⁻¹) i j) := by
      simpa [invGram] using
        TendstoInMeasure.pi_apply (TendstoInMeasure.pi_apply hInv i) j
    exact TendstoInMeasure.mul_limits_real
      (by simpa [s2] using hs2) hInvCoord</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Reassemble the coordinatewise limits into matrix convergence and unfold the estimator/target names.">refine tendstoInMeasure_pi (μ := μ) (fun i => ?_)
refine tendstoInMeasure_pi (μ := μ) (fun j => ?_)
simpa [olsHomoskedasticCovarianceStar, homoskedasticAsymptoticCovariance,
  s2, invGram, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul] using hEntry i j</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

## Downstream uses

The homoskedastic covariance result feeds the homoskedastic versions of the
Chapter 7 t and Wald statistics. The robust covariance page replaces
$\sigma^2 Q^{-1}$ with the sandwich target $Q^{-1}\Omega Q^{-1}$.
